name: Cafe de la Paix
summary: Mapped Luchon cafe fallback on Allées d'Étigny for the Pyrenees corridor; not verified as specialty coffee.
whyItMatters: The Arreau/Tourmalet corridor has sparse specialty-coffee evidence, so Luchon may be a practical stop if driving through or basing nearby.
pros: Mapped cafe in a known Luchon central promenade area
Potentially useful on a Pyrenees logistics day when options are limited
Luchon is a notable town in the broader Tourmalet/Pyrenees access region
cons: No sourced evidence found that this is excellent or specialty coffee
May be irrelevant if final Tourmalet approach does not pass through Luchon

logistics.distance_from_base: Useful only if staying in or transiting through Luchon; Arreau distance is significant
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not close to Col du Tourmalet; corridor fallback only
logistics.road_closure_notes: Tourmalet day access restrictions require separate viewing logistics research

timing.best_visit_window: Morning on a Pyrenees transfer or reconnaissance day
timing.race_day_risk: Medium to high depending on Stage 6 closure and final access route

sources.title: OpenStreetMap Cafe de la Paix, Bagnères-de-Luchon
sources.note: Supports mapped existence and coordinates; does not establish coffee quality or hours.

sources.title: Bagnères-de-Luchon background
sources.note: Supports Luchon's location and relevance as a Pyrenees resort/access town, not coffee quality.

open_questions: Verify whether any better specialty coffee exists in Arreau, Bagnères-de-Bigorre, La Mongie, or Luchon.
Confirm if the final Stage 6 approach makes Luchon relevant at all.
